summary: The reproduction starts a local D-Tale instance, enumerates a dataset ID, stores an attacker-controlled outlier filter, and triggers server-side pandas query execution that writes a proof file from the application process.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the attacker-controlled outlier-filter query to execute a shell command in the D-Tale process and write the string evohunt_rce into reproduction/proof.txt.
actual_observed_result: The run script created a local virtual environment, installed the target repository, started D-Tale on 127.0.0.1, enumerated the dataset ID through /dtale/process-keys, stored the malicious outlier filter through /dtale/save-column-filter/<id>, and triggered /dtale/data-export/<id>. The HTTP response returned a pandas evaluation error after execution, and reproduction/proof.txt contained evohunt_rce.
verification_method: The PoC verifies that the exact proof string appears in reproduction/proof.txt after the HTTP trigger path runs. It also checks that /dtale/process-keys and /dtale/save-column-filter/<id> succeed before the trigger request, distinguishing real server-side execution from an unrelated local file write.
evidence_produced_by_run: The unauthenticated /dtale/process-keys request returned a live dataset identifier
The trigger request to /dtale/data-export/<id> returned the pandas scalar-bool evaluation error after executing the payload
limitations_or_blockers: The chosen pandas expression performs its side effect before raising a pandas evaluation error, so the trigger request fails closed after command execution instead of returning filtered data.
